title: euphrasia minima proles pallida
intro: Euphrasia minima proles pallida, also known as dwarf eyebright, is a subspecies of the Euphrasia minima plant, which belongs to the gentian family and is commonly found in alpine and subalpine regions. This plant has been used traditionally in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating eye irritations and infections. The health benefits of Euphrasia minima proles pallida include its ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reduce swelling, and alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis and other eye infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area to promote healing and relaxation.
